scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present. Granny's Bed and Breakfast. August Booth is in his room, fumbling to get out of bed. After a great deal of difficulty and crashing into his dresser, he makes his way to a desk. He picks up the turns dial phone and dials only three numbers into it.

dialog.dialog: Hey, you there?  Good. This is taking too long; we need to accelerate the plan.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Outside Mr. Gold Pawnbroker & Antiquities Dealer. August is speaking with Henry, hiding behind a bunch of crates.

dialog.dialog: It's almost nine. You all set? You know what to do?
Operation Cobra is always ready. I just…
You just what?
I don't understand what this has to do with getting Emma to believe.
Sometimes other priorities assert themselves. Can you handle a little improvisation?
Yeah. Can you?
We're a go.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mr. Gold Pawnbroker & Antiquities Dealer. The bell of the front door rings as Henry walks inside. Mr. Gold emerges from the back room, confused by Henry's appearance.

dialog.dialog: Hey, MrGold!
Good morning, Henry. What can I do for you?
I wanna get a gift for Miss Blanchard.
Oh, I see.
Since… she didn't kill that woman.
Good thinking.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. August sneaks in through the back door to the back room while Henry and Mr. Gold talk, their word illegible. He closes the door silently and begins wandering around the room. Suddenly, Mr. Gold walks into the room.

dialog.dialog: Can I help you?
Yeah… I'm looking for some maps. I'm a bit of a collector.
Yes… well, there's maps through in the shop; this is my office.
I thought this was the entrance..
It's not. The shops are in there.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Storybrooke General Hospital. Kathryn Nolan is awake and being assessed by Dr. Whale. Whale notices Emma Swan standing outside.

dialog.dialog: Emma. Come on in. Look who's awake.
Kathryn, hi. Listen, I don't wanna take a lot of your time, but do you remember what happened?

I don't know much. I was in a car accident and  I remember the airbag going off.  And the next thing I knew, I was in the dark. In some basement. I didn't see anyone, but there was food and water. And then, I guess I was drugged?
Yeah, we are still trying to flush that out of your system.
And then I woke up  in a field at the edge of town  and I started walking. That was it.
You saw no one? You didn't hear a voice? Smell perfume?  Cologne? Anything?
Nothing, no. I'm sorry. I wish I could help. Especially since- While I was gone you thought I was dead?
Your DNA matched the heart we found.
They're grilling everybody down at the hospital lab to see who doctored the DNA results.
Why would anyone do this?
I think somebody was trying to frame Mary Margaret.
But why? Who would do something like that?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mr. Gold's shop.

dialog.dialog: You broke our deal.
I broke one deal in my life, dear - and it, sadly, wasn't this one.
Kathryn was supposed to die and Mary Margaret was to get the blame.

Yeah, murder seems so much worse here though, doesn't it? You can't just turn someone into a snail and step on them, can you? You didn't say "kill her". We agreed that something tragic should happen to her. Now, abduction is tragic.
The intent was perfectly clear.
Oh, let's not talk about intent. Intent is meaningless.
Intent is everything.
Please.
This is going to raise all kinds of questions about where she was and how the test's results were fake.
Oh, yes. And um, and who put the key in her cell.
It's all gonna lead to me, isn't it? You bastard! This isn't making any sense. You and I - we've been in this together. From the start.
Oh, have we?
You created the curse for me. The curse that brought us here and built all this.
Yes, it's about time you said "thank you."
Why did you do it?
Well, you are a smart woman, your Majesty. Figure it out.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Past. A small village. Several children play together. Baelfire plays with a ball. Kicking it against a wall, the ball is knocked into the path of a donkey cart. When Baelfire goes to retrieve it, he trips and scrapes his knee.

dialog.dialog: Hey. Hey! What are you doing in the middle of the road, boy?
I'm sorry. I-I…
Hey, I know you. It's fine. It's fine. It was the donkey's fault. You want a chicken? Or some eggs?
It's alright, no. I should probably just…
What's going on?
It's nothing. It was my fault. I wasn't looking where I was going. But, he says he's fine!
I'm fine, Papa. Really.
Are you sure, Bae?
Yes. I'm fine.
Well, I suppose it won't happen again.
It won't.
No. No.
What's that?
It's nothing.
It's nothing.
Don't. Bother.
No, Papa. No. Please, Papa, don't. No, Papa! Papa!

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Storybrooke General Hospital. David Nolan approaches Kathryn's hospital bed. He goes to kiss her but she wakes up.

dialog.dialog: I'm sorry.
What are you doing?
I was trying to kiss you on your forehead. It was meant to be sweet.
Well thank you. It's good to see you.
Kathryn, I'm sorry. I'm sorry I lied to you. I'm sorry I cheated on you. I'm just so sorry for all of this.
David, it's okay. You know what we had… it wasn't it for you. Maybe for both of us. I can't blame you for just being the first one to see it.
You are uh… kind of amazing.
Yeah I am.  Now go on. Get out of here so I can get some rest.
I'm going to give you that kiss on the forehead now.
Knock yourself out.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. The Blanchard loft. Granny enters Mary Margaret's welcome home party.

dialog.dialog: Hi!
Hey! How are you? Would you like some?
All of these people… just to welcome me home?
You've got a lot of friends.
Didn't feel like that yesterday.  Here you go.
Thank you.
(Looking at MrGold. Speaks to August.) How bad was it?
Getting caught in his office? Not bad. I played it off.
What were you looking for? Did you find it?
Nope. But I have a feeling it's going to find me.  There she is. Why don't you give her your present?
Hey, I have something for you.
Thank you.  We're so glad you didn't kill MrsNolan.
It's from the whole class.  And I got you a bell.
Thank you. Tell everyone I'll be back soon.
Okay.
Henry, we should get you home before your mom finds out. That won't be pretty.
Hey. Hey Henry. Leaving already?
Yeah. Gotta get home and do homework.
She's kinda tired. I think if you just give it some time.
I just wanted to-
Hey Henry, why don't you head home with David?
Okay.
Sorry.
Okay.
Hard to let him go, isn't it? Your son.
Yeah. Pretty much the hardest thing. Speaking of something we weren't talking about, was it you?
Was what me?
Did you make Kathryn suddenly materialize? 'Cause it sure played that way to me. Was that the magic you were going to work? Because if you kidnapped that poor, innocent woman just to let her go-
Are you proposing I'm working with Regina or against her?
I don't know. Maybe diagonally.
Well you keep working on that one. My question's about something else. What do you know about him?
Goes by August. He's a writer. Typewriter wrapped in an enigma wrapped in stubble. Why?
He was poking around my shop today. August Wayne Booth, clearly a false name. There's one thing I know about, it's names.
Writers go by pseudonyms. What does it matter?
You trust him?
Yeah. A lot more than I trust you.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Past. Rumplestiltskin and Baelfire enter their house. Honora cleans up the room. Rumplestiltskin turns to face the maid.

dialog.dialog: Thank you, Honora. You can fetch us supper now, dearie.
You killed that man.
Well, you were hurt. Speaking of which -
No, I don't won't magic. It's just a scrape.
This will heal it.
So will this.
As you wish.
You're different now. You see it, don't you? You hurt people all the time.
I created a truce in the Ogre War, Bae. I walked into the field of battle and I made it stop. I let the children home. Surely, a man who's saved a thousand lives -
is done. A man who's saved a thousand lives can be done with it. You can stop doing things.
I can't. I need more power so I can protect you.
I wouldn't need protecting if you didn't have power.
Well, I can't get rid of it.
Have you tried?
Tried? If someone kills me  with this, then they gain the power. Now you know that, Bae. Is that what you want?
That's not what I want. I just think there might be other ways  to get rid of the power. Have you looked for - (They notice Honora. Rumplestiltskin sheathes the dagger).
Well, you look for other ways, Bae. But don't get your hopes up.
Papa, if I find a way for you to get rid of the power… A way that doesn't kill you or hurt me, would you do it?
It's not possible.
If it was, would you do it?
Are you really that unhappy, Bae? I conjure anything you desire. Name it. What do you want?
I want my father.
All I want is your happiness, Bae. If you find a way, I'll do it.
Good.  The deal is struck.
Struck.

scenes.sceneDesc: Present. Granny's Diner. Emma enters the diner and sits down opposite of Sidney Glass who’s enjoying a coffee.

dialog.dialog: Hey Sidney.
Emma: Hey. So, things certainly did work out, didn't they? For your friend?
You told me you could help me with Mary Margaret and I wanted to believe you. But, eventually, there are things that even a blind sheriff  cannot ignore.
Is that a bug?
Oh, for God's sake, Sidney, drop it. You fooled me. You spied on me. And you reported it all back to that sick, crazy woman. I can't even imagine what she has on you but it must be something huge.
She's a good mayor.

She tried to get Mary Margaret convicted of a murder that didn't even happen. You're in a lot of trouble. There's a DNA trail in the basement of some house out there and I'm gonna find it. And she's going to go away.
Maybe. But, I wouldn't bet against her. She's an amazing woman.

Do you (she casts Sidney a long look) Are you in love with her? Fine.  Whatever. Here's the thing: Before you know it I'll have that evidence. And you need to think long and hard. You can either help me and help yourself or you can go down with her, too.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Past. In the woods some kids playfully practice sword fighting.

dialog.dialog: Why don't you join in, Bae? I have some business nearby that would bore you.
Alright.
Baelfire.
Careful, Morraine. You don't wanna be seen with me. I'm dangerous.
They're just scared of your papa. But I'm not. You won't let him hurt me. I don't think he's so bad anyway. I saw him on the battlefield when he stopped the fighting. It was like a miracle.
Now he's getting worse every day. But he said he'll change back if I find a way. I just don't know where to look.
Reul Ghorm.
What?
I heard about it when I was in the trenches. The other soldiers talked about it. Reul Ghorm is an ancient being that rules the night. The original power.
Bigger than papa? Worse than papa?
Bigger than anything.  He's coming back. Good luck to you.
Your friend didn't want to say hallo?
You frighten them.
What is there to be frightened of, Bae? They'll get over that in time.
You have strains on your boots.
Oh, yes. That. We need a new maid.
Gods, no!
She heard us talking about the knife.
She was mute. She couldn't tell anyone.
Even mutes can draw a picture.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present. The convent. In the distance August talks to Mother Superior. Mr. Gold is waiting for her as she goes back inside.

dialog.dialog: Mother Superior. Good afternoon.
Our rent is paid in full.
I'm not here about the rent.
Well, good day to you then.
Tell me, that man who just left here - who did he say he was? What did he want?
I don't have to tell you that.
And I don't have to not double your rent. What did he want?
Advice and council. He came to town looking for his father after a long separation and he recently found him.
Ah. And a happy reunion has already taken place?
No,he hasn't spoken to him yet.
And why not?
Mhm, it was a difficult parting. There are many issues to be resolved between them.
I see.

scenes.sceneDesc: Enchanted Forest. Past. In the woods. Baelfire carefully walks up to a tree and sits down.

dialog.dialog: Reul Ghorm? Are you there? If you can help me, please make yourself known to me. ) Can you help me?
I can help.
How do I know I can trust you?
Because there's good magic and dark magic. And I'm on the right side.
You're a fairy.
And you are not untouched by magic, are you, child? There's something dark in your life.
My father. He's the Dark One.
I can't make him the man he was before. But I can send him some place where we won't be able to use his powers.
Not a jail. I want to be with him. Like it used to be.
Not a jail, young one, just a place without magic.
Magic is everywhere.
In this world, yes. You see, what ails your father is specific to our realm. His powers do not belong here. You must go where you can escape that wretched curse.
Go? We have to leave?
Yes, it is the only way. Can you do it? Can you leave everything here behind for the unknown?
If it means I'll get my father back, then yes.
You're a very good son, Baelfire. You are the part of him that keeps him human. That little light inside of him that still glows. It's his love for you. Hold out your hand.
What is it?
A magic bean. The very last one that is known to our kind. The others got away from us.  You just use it wisely and follow wherever it leads you. It'll save you both.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present. David approaches Mary Margaret.

dialog.dialog: Mary Margaret.  Please wait. Look, I'll leave if you want. I just think we need to talk.
So, talk.
I need to apologize.
Yes, you do. Keep going.
I didn't believe you. I didn't stand with you.
You know, I'll never forget that moment. The moment sort of blows you backwards and the one person you thought would always be there to catch you - he isn't there.
Look at what was going on: your jewelry box. Your fingerprints. Knife in your apartment.
It was a setup.
And a really good one. I'm human. I fell for it. I'm sorry but we have to move forward.
But we can't. It's like something in this world doesn't want us together.
Like what? Dark forces?

Maybe. I don't know. But it's like something just keeps pouring poison between us. And what I don't want, is to have all of those good memories replaced by moments like that. When I looked at you… And I saw you didn't believe me
No. I know. I'm so sorry.
I know.  I know.
I love you.
And that is what makes it all so sad.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Rumplestiltskin’s house. Rumplestiltskin is spinning. Baelfire enters.

dialog.dialog: Papa. Papa.  I found it. I found a way for things to be like they were. I want you to come with me. I can make things right. Have you heard of the Reul Ghorm?
The Blue Star. The Blue Fairy. Oh, son, please tell me you didn't. Fairy magic doesn't mix well with what I am.
You promised. She can help us. To take us to a place without magic.
A place without magic? (standing up) I'd be powerless. Weak.
Like everyone else. It wouldn't matter. We'd be happy.
We could be happy here.
Father, please. You're getting worse. And you promised. This can work. It can. You made a deal with me. Are you backing out?
No.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present. Hopper Psychiatry Office. Mr. Gold knocks once and turns to leave. Archie Hopper opens the door. He notices Mr. Gold.

dialog.dialog: MrGold? Are you here for the rent?
Why does everyone ask that?
Oh, because you- Never mind.  Would you- Would you like to talk?
I don't know.
Oh, um, if you like to get something off your chest, please come in.

scenes.sceneDesc: Present. Storybrooke. Hopper Psychiatry Office.

dialog.dialog: A son? I- I didn't know you had a son. How old is he?
Let's start with something easier.
Okay. Um, what do you mean to say that you may have found him?
Let's just say there's someone acting the way I would expect him to act.
So, you recognize him?
Maybe.  Perhaps I'm just seeing what I wanna see. I don't know.
Okay. Well, wouldn't he recognize you?
There was conflict. I'm not sure he's ready for a tear soaked reunion.
So, he sought you out and he's hanging back? Maybe, he's watching to see if he's welcome. Looking for a sign that all is forgiven.
No, no, no. He's not the one that needs to be…  I think he might still be very angry.
Anger between a parent and a child is the most natural thing in the world.
I think he might be here to try to kill me.
Ah. Right. That's- That's not.
I let him go. I’ve spent my entire life since trying to fix it. And now he's finally here… And I just don't know what to do.
Be honest. Just tell him what you told me, and ask him for forgiveness. And when you're face-to-face, you'll know what to do.
Honesty has never been the best color of me.
There's no other way.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present. In the woods. Mr. Gold confronts August Booth.

dialog.dialog: I know who you are. And I know what you are looking for.
Well, then, I guess all the lying can stop.  Papa.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Past. Rumplestiltskin and Baelfire walk in the woods

dialog.dialog: Where are we going, boy? What kind of world is this we’re going to? What kind of world is without magic?
A better one.
Oh my gods, boy. It's like a tornado.
We have to go through.
No, no. I don't think I can.
We must. It's the only way.
No, no, no, no, no. It's a trick. It'll tear us apart.
It's not. It'll be okay. I promise.  Papa, we have to go through. What are you doing? Papa, it won't stay open long. Let go.
I can't. I can't.
Papa, please, it's the only way we can be together.
No, Bae. I can't.
Papa, please.
I can't.
You coward! You promised. Don't break our deal.
I have to.
Papa!
Bae? Bae! No, no, no, no, no. No, no, Bae. I'm sorry, Bae!  I want to come with you! I want to come with you, Bae! I want to come with you! Bae! Bae! Bae!

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present. Somewhere in the woods. His back turned to Mr. Gold August stands a few yards away.

dialog.dialog: You were right, Bae. You were always right. I was a coward and I never should have let you go. I know, it's little consolation but I just want you to know that ever since you left, ever since you crossed the barriers of time in space in every waking moment (MrGold's voice breaks) I’ve been looking for you. And now that I’ve finally found you… I know I can't make up for the past, for the lost time. All I can do,  is to ask you to do what you've always done. And that's to be the bigger man… And forgive me.  I'm so sorry, son. I'm so sorry, Bae.  Oh, my boy. My beautiful boy. Can you truly, truly forgive me?
I forgive you, Papa.
You were looking for the knife.
I thought, if you still had it, it would mean that you hadn't changed.
Well, let's go and find it and see.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present. Elsewhere in the woods. August digs up the dagger.

dialog.dialog: I buried it here shortly after Emma came to town. Things were changing. Didn't want to take the chance of Regina finding it.
Of course.
It should be right about here, son. Here. Look.  I want you to take it. Destroy it, the way I know you always wanted to. I found you, and I don't need it anymore. I chose it once. Now, I choose you.
It's remarkable.  By the power of the darkness I command thee, Dark One.
You're trying to control me?
I command thee, Dark One.
You're not my son. You're not Baelfire.
Papa, why would you say that? I'm just trying to use your power to help us.

Enough! It's over, Booth. Or whoever you are. My son would never try to use me. And he would know, that this knife cannot harness any magic in this world, because there is no magic in this world. He didn't want me  dabbling.
So, why bury a useless knife?
Oh, I wouldn't say it was useless. It still cuts through flesh rather nicely. It's about time you start answering some questions, sunshine. Why the theatrics? Why didn't you just come to me?
I needed you to work for it. I needed you to want it so bad, you would ignore what your eyes were seeing. Do I even look like him at all?
How did you know about this knife?
I hear things.
No one here knows about this knife.
No one here remembers.
And yet you do. You're from there, aren't you? From my world?
The fact that you're asking the question means you know the answer.
Now, that's that settled-  How about my other question? Who told you about me and the knife?
A little fairy.
Why did you want it? If you know who I am, then you know who I am. The chances of you surviving this little encounter are pretty slim. So, why take the risk?
Because I'll die anyway.
What?
I'm sick. I'm sick and I need magic. I was going to get the saviour to believe. But that woman  I don't think I'm going to make it long enough to see that happen.
She trusts you. It might be enough. Try again.
You're gonna let me live?
You're gonna die either way. This way at least I might get something out of it.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Past. Alone, Rumplestiltskin stands in the woods.

dialog.dialog: Reul Ghorm! Show yourself!  How do I follow him?
You had the way you didn't take it. And there are no more magic beans.
That's a lie.
We don't do that.
A lie.
You'll never make it to that world.
Oh, I'll find a way. There must be other paths. A realm jumper?
No.
A time turner?
No.
A mage?
There is no-
A curse?
No.
Ah, so it is a curse.
Of course you would think of a curse instead of a blessing. Your magic is limited by its own rotten core, Rumplestiltskin. Anyway, it can't be done. Not without a great price.
I’ve already paid a great price.
So, you'd be willing to sacrifice this world for the next? Because that's how great the price is.
Well, what do you think?
Well, then I'll comfort myself knowing that such a curse is beyond your abilities.
Oh, for now. But I’ve got all the time in the world. I will do nothing else, I will love nothing else. I will find a way. You took my son, but I will get him back.
I didn't take your son.
You took my son, but I will get him back!
You drove him away.
I will find him! I will find him! I will find him!

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present. The Sheriff’s office. Regina awaits Emma.

dialog.dialog: Congratulations, Sheriff Swan. There's about to be a big break in your case. You just got yourself a confession. But, I want you to listen to the whole explanation, so you understand why this happened.
I'll hang on every word you say.
Sidney, you can come in now.  Tell her what you told me.

It was me. I confess. I abducted Kathryn, and I held her in the basement of an abandoned summer home by the lake. I bribed a lab tech to get me the heart from the hospital, and I used that same person to doctor the lab results.
And the other thing?
I  borrowed some skeleton keys from Regina and  planted the knife in your apartment.
My keys. Can't help to feel personally violated by that part.
I am supposed to believe you did this for why now?

I was going to find her after the conviction, be a hero. Then, get the inside track on the biggest story to ever hit this town. I'd get my job back. Plus, a novel, and a movie, and (pauses again) I don't know. It sounds crazy now.
I don't know about crazy. But false, yes. False as hell.
I have maps to where the house is. You'll find chains in the basement. Lots of fingerprints, I'm sure – hers and mine. But, I didn't hurt her.
The man has obviously suffered some kind of mental break. He clearly hasn't been himself for a while.
Yeah. It's like his words aren't his at all.
Wow. You are so sold on your own rush to judgment that you can't even see the truth anymore.
A word in the hallway, please.  Well, that's the biggest load of crap I’ve ever heard.
I'm pretty sure that's not true.

That poor man. I know, you're behind all this. I understand that you own the game, and you've set the boards so that no one else can win. But I am about to start playing an entirely different game.  I don't care about what happens to you. I don't care about what happens to me. All I care about is my kid. And you are going to leave him alone.
Am I?
Uh-uh. I'm talking. You're a sociopath, lady. You tried to take away someone that I love. And now… I'm going to take away someone you love. I am taking back my son.
